name: water
explanation: Neutral solvent.

name: glycerin
explanation: Humectant, completely safe.

name: squalane
explanation: Stable hydrocarbon oil, Malassezia cannot metabolize it.

name: aloe barbadensis leaf juice
explanation: Soothing extract, safe.

name: hyaluronic acid
explanation: Powerful humectant, highly FA safe.

name: niacinamide
explanation: Vitamin B3, safe and helps regulate sebum.

name: butylene glycol
explanation: Safe solvent and penetration enhancer.

name: propylene glycol
explanation: Safe humectant and solvent.

name: caprylic/capric triglyceride
explanation: MCT, Malassezia cannot consume chains shorter than C11.

name: mineral oil
explanation: Refined hydrocarbon, safe for FA.

name: isopropyl myristate
explanation: Powerful FA trigger (C14 chain).

name: isopropyl palmitate
explanation: Powerful FA trigger (C16 chain).

name: polysorbate 20
explanation: Contains lauric acid (C12) which feeds Malassezia.

name: polysorbate 40
explanation: Contains palmitic acid (C16), feeds Malassezia.

name: polysorbate 60
explanation: Contains stearic acid (C18), strong FA food.

name: polysorbate 80
explanation: Contains oleic acid (C18:1), potent FA trigger.

name: lauric acid
explanation: C12 fatty acid, highly specific food for Malassezia.

name: myristic acid
explanation: C14 fatty acid, feeds FA.

name: palmitic acid
explanation: C16 fatty acid, known Malassezia food.

name: stearic acid
explanation: C18 fatty acid, supports fungal growth.

name: oleic acid
explanation: C18:1, one of the primary preferred foods for Malassezia.

name: linoleic acid
explanation: C18:2 can still be metabolized by many strains.

name: linolenic acid
explanation: Unsaturated long fatty acid that feeds FA.

name: coconut oil
explanation: Rich in lauric acid (C12), extremely dangerous for FA.

name: cocos nucifera (coconut) oil
explanation: Scientific name for Coconut Oil. Rich in lauric acid (C12).

name: olive oil
explanation: Predominantly oleic acid (C18:1), strong trigger.

name: olea europaea (olive) fruit oil
explanation: Botanical name for Olive Oil. Predominantly oleic acid.

name: jojoba oil
explanation: Contains long-chain esters that feed Malassezia.

name: simmondsia chinensis (jojoba) seed oil
explanation: Botanical name for Jojoba Oil.

name: argan oil
explanation: High in oleic and linoleic acids.

name: rosehip oil
explanation: Contains linoleic and linolenic acids, potential trigger.

name: sunflower seed oil
explanation: High in linoleic and oleic acids.

name: sweet almond oil
explanation: Rich in oleic acid.

name: shea butter
explanation: Contains stearic and oleic acids.

name: cocoa butter
explanation: High oleic and stearic acids.

name: galactomyces ferment filtrate
explanation: Yeast ferments exacerbate fungal overgrowth.

name: bifida ferment lysate
explanation: Fermented ingredients can act as a food source for malassezia.

name: saccharomyces ferment filtrate
explanation: Yeast-based ingredient that triggers FA.

name: mangifera indica (mango) seed butter
explanation: Contains stearic and oleic acid.

name: decyl oleate
explanation: An ester of oleic acid and decyl alcohol, feeds FA.

name: ethylhexyl palmitate
explanation: An ester of palmitic acid, highly comedogenic and feeds FA.

name: glyceryl stearate
explanation: An ester of glycerin and stearic acid, known FA trigger.

name: glyceryl stearate se
explanation: Self-emulsifying form of glyceryl stearate, feeds FA.

name: sorbitan stearate
explanation: Ester of sorbitol and stearic acid.

name: sorbitan olivate
explanation: Ester of olive fatty acids, problematic for some.

name: cetearyl olivate
explanation: Often combined with sorbitan olivate, derived from olive oil, can feed FA.

name: peg-100 stearate
explanation: PEG ester of stearic acid.

name: macadamia ternifolia seed oil
explanation: High in oleic acid and palmitoleic acid.

name: persea gratissima (avocado) oil
explanation: Contains oleic and linoleic acids.

name: butyrospermum parkii (shea) butter
explanation: Botanical name for shea butter, feeds FA.

name: theobroma cacao (cocoa) seed butter
explanation: High in oleic and stearic acids.

name: vitis vinifera (grape) seed oil
explanation: Contains high linoleic acid, which is still a C18 FA.

name: ricinus communis (castor) seed oil
explanation: Contains ricinoleic acid (C18), can be FA food.

name: prunus amygdalus dulcis (sweet almond) oil
explanation: Contains oleic and linoleic acids.

name: sesamum indicum (sesame) seed oil
explanation: High in oleic and linoleic acids.

name: borago officinalis seed oil
explanation: Contains gamma-linolenic acid (C18), feeds FA.

name: oenothera biennis (evening primrose) oil
explanation: Contains linoleic and gamma-linolenic acids.

name: helianthus annuus (sunflower) seed oil
explanation: Commonly contains high levels of linoleic and oleic acids.

name: carthamus tinctorius (safflower) seed oil
explanation: High in linoleic acid.

name: cannabis sativa (hemp) seed oil
explanation: Contains linoleic and linolenic acids.

name: glycine soja (soybean) oil
explanation: Contains high levels of linoleic acid.

name: triticum vulgare (wheat) germ oil
explanation: Extremely highly comedogenic, contains triggering fatty acids.

name: cetearyl alcohol
explanation: Fatty alcohol, generally FA safe, though some may be sensitive.

name: cetyl alcohol
explanation: Fatty alcohol, generally safe for FA.

name: stearyl alcohol
explanation: Fatty alcohol, safe for FA.

name: myristyl myristate
explanation: Ester of myristic acid, potent trigger.

name: isopropyl isostearate
explanation: Ester of isostearic acid, feeds FA.

name: diisostearyl malate
explanation: Ester of isostearyl alcohol, triggers FA.

name: sorbitan oleate
explanation: Ester of oleic acid.

name: sorbitan sesquioleate
explanation: Ester of oleic acid.

name: glycol stearate
explanation: Ester of stearic acid.

name: cholesterol
explanation: Lipid that some strains of Malassezia can metabolize.

name: ceramide 3
explanation: Safe lipid type for FA.

name: ceramide np
explanation: Safe ceramide, barrier repair.

name: panthenol
explanation: Vitamin B5, soothing and completely FA safe.

name: allantoin
explanation: Soothing agent, does not feed FA.

name: centella asiatica extract
explanation: Healing extract, highly safe.

name: snail secretion filtrate
explanation: Hydrating and repairing, FA safe.

name: propolis extract
explanation: Antibacterial, soothing, does not feed FA.

name: beta-glucan
explanation: Soothing humectant, safe.

name: salicylic acid
explanation: BHA, actually helps clear out pores and can suppress FA.

name: glycolic acid
explanation: AHA, safe for FA.

name: lactic acid
explanation: AHA, hydrating and safe for FA.

name: azelaic acid
explanation: Anti-inflammatory, anti-microbial, can treat FA.

name: urea
explanation: Keratolytic and humectant, helps treat FA.

name: dimethicone
explanation: Silicone, biologically inert and FA safe.

name: cyclopentasiloxane
explanation: Volatile silicone, completely safe.

name: peg-40 hydrogenated castor oil
explanation: Hydrogenated castor oil can sometimes be a problem.

name: peg-60 hydrogenated castor oil
explanation: Generally considered FA safe due to full ethoxylation, unlike non-hydrogenated.

name: carbomer
explanation: Thickening agent, completely safe.

name: xanthan gum
explanation: Natural thickener, safe.

name: phenoxyethanol
explanation: Preservative, safe.

name: ethylhexylglycerin
explanation: Preservative booster, safe.

name: methylparaben
explanation: Preservative, safe.

name: propylparaben
explanation: Preservative, safe.

name: disodium edta
explanation: Chelating agent, safe.

name: fragrance
explanation: Safe for FA, but can be generally irritating.

name: parfum
explanation: Safe for FA, but can be generally irritating.

name: tocopherol
explanation: Vitamin E, antioxidant, safe.

name: tocopheryl acetate
explanation: Vitamin E derivative, safe.

name: ascorbic acid
explanation: Vitamin C, safe.

name: sodium hyaluronate
explanation: Salt form of HA, safe.

name: zinc pca
explanation: Helps control sebum, anti-microbial, very safe.
